data.content:
Here's the analysis of MSBuild evaluation performance issues in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Nested Import Chain

```
EvalHeavy.csproj → level1.props → level2.props → level3.props
```

MSBuild must parse and evaluate every imported file **during the evaluation phase** before any target can execute. Each import adds XML parsing, property/item evaluation, and condition checking overhead. Three levels deep is unnecessary here — all three `.props` files just set simple properties and could be flattened into one file or inlined.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```

The `**\*.*` glob forces MSBuild to **recursively scan the entire directory tree** during evaluation. It only excludes `bin` and `obj`, so it will still crawl into `.git/`, `node_modules/`, `packages/`, and any other large directories. This is extremely expensive on repos with many files. Fix by:

- Restricting to specific extensions: `**\*.cs;**\*.json`
- Adding exclusions via `$(DefaultItemExcludes)` which already filters `.git`, etc.
- Scoping to `src\**\*.*` instead of the entire tree

### 3. File I/O Property Function During Evaluation

```xml
<BuildNotes Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```

This calls `System.IO.File.ReadAllText()` **during the evaluation phase**. MSBuild evaluation is meant to resolve properties and items quickly; performing disk I/O here blocks the evaluator. The evaluation phase runs before any targets — so this cost is paid on every build, restore, and even IDE project load. This should be moved to a target in the **execution phase** using `ReadLinesFromFile` or a custom task, so it only runs when actually needed.

### 4. Evaluation Phase vs. Execution Phase

MSBuild works in two phases:

| Phase | What happens | Cost sensitivity |
|---|---|---|
| **Evaluation** | Parses XML, resolves imports, expands globs, evaluates properties/conditions | Runs on every load — must be fast |
| **Execution** | Runs targets and tasks | Only runs during build |

All three issues above add work to the **evaluation phase**, meaning they slow down not just builds but also IDE operations like IntelliSense, solution load, and design-time builds.
